title: BDL surgery protocol
author: Cudalbu, Cristina
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
Protocol for bile duct ligation surgery (BDL) in adult and young (pups) rats performed in the  MRS4Brain research group  
  By:   
  Dr.med.vet. Stefanita-Octavian Mitrea (   stefan.mitrea@epfl.ch   ,  CIBM Center for Biomedical Imaging, Lausanne, Switzerland; Animal Imaging and Technology, Ecole Polytechnique Federale de Lausanne (EPFL), Lausanne, Switzerland)    
  Dario Sessa (   dario.sessa@epfl.ch   ,  Swiss Pediatric Liver Center, Department of Pediatrics, Gynecology and Obstetrics, University of Geneva, Geneva, Switzerland.) 
  after a protocol of Dr.Graziano Oldani  (Division of Abdominal and Transplantation Surgery, Department of Surgery, Faculty of Medicine, Geneva University Hospitals, Geneva, Switzerland; Faculty of Medicine, Hepato-pancreato-biliary Centre, Geneva University Hospitals, Geneva, Switzerland) 
 Please cite the following manuscripts if you ar using the protocol described herein: 
  For adult rats:  
  Longitudinal neurometabolic changes in the hippocampus of a rat model of chronic hepatic encephalopathy - PubMed (nih.gov)  
  Probiotics improve the neurometabolic profile of rats with chronic cholestatic liver disease - PubMed (nih.gov)  
  Probiotics combined with rifaximin influence the neurometabolic changes in a rat model of type C HE - PubMed (nih.gov)  
  PET CMRglc mapping and 1H-MRS show altered glucose uptake and neurometabolic profiles in BDL rats - PubMed (nih.gov)  
  Central nervous system and systemic oxidative stress interplay with inflammation in a bile duct ligation rat model of type C hepatic encephalopathy - PubMed (nih.gov)  
  For rat pups:  
  Longitudinal osmotic and neurometabolic changes in young rats with chronic cholestatic liver disease - PubMed (nih.gov)  
  Neurometabolic changes in a rat pup model of type C hepatic encephalopathy depend on age at liver disease onset - PubMed (nih.gov)
